setting.note: One man's love turned into an inventory of a city. The map runs from the wealthy boutiques and flats of Nişantaşı, where Kemal is engaged and the affair begins, down the hill through Beyoğlu to the old timber houses of Çukurcuma, where the years of longing settle and where Pamuk later built a real museum to hold them.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Orhan Pamuk, first published in Turkish as Masumiyet Müzesi in 2008. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Museum of Innocence, Orhan Pamuk (2008). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: A deep pomegranate red for the obsession and the vitrines of the museum. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper at 6.70:1.
